items.title: SEC Consult SA-20260617-0 :: Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities in Sprecher Automation SPRECON-E-C/-E-P/-E-T3
items.description:
Posted by SEC Consult Vulnerability Lab via Fulldisclosure on Jun 20 SEC Consult Vulnerability Lab Security Advisory < 20260617-0 > 
======================================================================= 
 title: Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities 
 product: Sprecher Automation SPRECON-E-C/-E-P/-E-T3 
 vulnerable version: See vulnerable versions below 
 fixed version: See solution section below 
 CVE number: CVE-2022-4333, CVE-2022-4332, CVE-2025-4
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] THREAT-INTEL advisory. Risk score 8.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items.ioc_note: Actor-attribution campaign — IOC set derived from threat actor research, not automated feed extraction.

apex.ai_summary: [P1] Threat Intel - high severity (Risk 8.5/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 2 indicators detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: CRITICAL PRIORITY - Patch within 24-48 hours. Activate IR playbook. Notify SOC lead and CISO.

ioc_paywall.message: 2 IOC(s) at 14.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — SEC Consult SA-20260617-0  Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities in Sprecher Automat
id: 3d75ea53-5743-5f6b-84d9-ff84199fb7c0
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2022-4333 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2022-4333
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/22
modified: 2026/06/22
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2022-4333 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-22T04:25:31Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2022-4333", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2022-4333 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:4653753; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-22, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CISA Adds LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Vulnerability to KEV List Following Active Exploitation
items.description:
CISA has added a critical LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-54420, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog following evidence of active exploitation in the wild. The flaw affects shared hosting environments and poses a significant risk to servers running CloudLinux with CageFS isolation. The vulnerability is classified as a UNIX symbolic link (symlink) […] 
 The post  CISA Adds LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Vulnerability to KEV List Following Active Exploitation  appear

items.title: U.S. CISA adds Splunk Enterprise flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and urges agencies to fix it by Sunday
items.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds Splunk Enterprise flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a Splunk Enterprise flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20253 (CVSS score of 9.8), to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. The flaw CVE-2026-20253 is an improper authentication vulnerability in the PostgreSQL sidecar service of […]

items.title: Critical WordPress Plugin Vulnerability Exposes 1 Million Sites to File Deletion Attacks
items.description:
A critical security vulnerability in the widely used Avada (Fusion) Builder WordPress plugin has exposed over 1 million websites to arbitrary file-deletion attacks, potentially leading to full-site compromise and remote code execution. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8713 with a CVSS score of 9.1, was discovered by security researcher “daroo” and reported through the Wordfence Bug […] 
 The post  Critical WordPress Plugin Vulnerability Exposes 1 Million Sites to File Deletion Attacks  appeared first on

items.title: Chrome Extensions’ Critical Flaws Let Attackers Easily Compromise Millions of Browsers
items.description:
Critical security flaws discovered in widely used Chrome extensions SiderAI and MaxAI are putting millions of users at risk, enabling attackers to fully compromise browser sessions and potentially access sensitive data across websites and local systems. Security researchers at Rebora Security uncovered vulnerabilities dubbed “Spyder” and “MaXSS” affecting AI-powered “agentic side panel” extensions. These tools, […]
